title: E2VID++: Higher-Resolution Event-to-Video Reconstruction with Transformers
abstract: Event cameras report asynchronous per-pixel brightness changes with microsecond latency, but converting their sparse stream back into a frame-based video remains the de facto gateway for downstream computer-vision pipelines. We introduce E2VID++, a transformer-based event-to-video reconstruction network that operates at 720p in real time on a single GPU. By combining a streaming attention module with a learned temporal denoiser we push state-of-the-art reconstruction PSNR by 1.8 dB on the ECD and MVSEC benchmarks. Code and pretrained weights are released.

title: DSEC-Flow: A Large-Scale Driving Dataset for Event-Based Optical Flow
abstract: We release DSEC-Flow, a 12-hour driving dataset captured with a stereo Prophesee Gen-4 event camera rig synchronized to a frame camera. Pixel-accurate optical flow ground truth is generated via a high-precision LiDAR reprojection pipeline. DSEC-Flow contains 1.2 million labeled event-flow pairs across day, night, tunnel, and rain conditions — 4× the size of MVSEC. We benchmark seven existing event-based optical flow methods and show that performance degrades by 32% under low-light conditions where DSEC-Flow is largest. Dataset, evaluation server, and baseline checkpoints are public.

title: Asynchronous Spiking Object Detection on Loihi 2 for Drone Collision Avoidance
abstract: We deploy a fully asynchronous spiking object detector on Intel's Loihi 2 neuromorphic chip and integrate it with a 250-gram quadrotor for end-to-end on-board collision avoidance. The network ingests raw DVS events at 1 MHz, predicts bounding boxes through a spiking YOLO-style head, and emits motor commands every 4 ms. Closed-loop flight tests in cluttered indoor environments show a 92% obstacle-avoidance success rate at 6 m/s — a 38% improvement over the strongest GPU baseline at 8× lower power. We make all chip configurations public.

title: Spike-Aware Self-Supervised Pretraining for Event-Based Vision
abstract: Event-camera data is plentiful but mostly unlabeled. We introduce SpikeMAE, a masked-event autoencoder that pretrains on 1.4 billion unlabeled events from public driving datasets. After fine-tuning, SpikeMAE improves downstream event-based object detection (+4.1 mAP), gesture recognition (+3.2 acc), and optical flow (-0.8 EPE) over from-scratch baselines. We study the interaction between event-rate, masking ratio, and downstream gains and release a public pretraining recipe. Code at github.com/eccg/spike-mae.

title: EventSLAM-GS: Event-Based SLAM with 3D Gaussian Splatting
abstract: We present EventSLAM-GS, the first event-based SLAM system that builds a dense 3D Gaussian-splatting map online from a single DVS camera. By coupling event-rate-adaptive tracking with a differentiable Gaussian renderer we recover both pose and a photometrically consistent map without intensity frames. On TUM-VI-EVS our trajectories match frame-based ORB-SLAM3 within 6% RMSE while reconstructing 1.4 M Gaussians at 10 Hz. Source code released.

title: Towards a Tier-1 Spike Camera: 100-kfps Asynchronous Imaging at 6.5 megapixels
abstract: We report a 6.5-megapixel spike-driven imaging sensor that emits per-pixel asynchronous spike trains at an effective rate of 100,000 frames/s under daylight illumination. Each pixel uses a Pulse-Frequency-Modulation readout coupled to a 4T charge-domain reset. We characterize dynamic range (>140 dB), motion blur, and end-to-end latency on a drone obstacle-avoidance benchmark and outperform a Prophesee Gen-4 sensor by 18 ms at equal accuracy.

title: v2e-Lite: Energy-Efficient Frame-to-Event Conversion for Edge Deployments
abstract: Synthetic event generation from RGB video is widely used to bootstrap event-camera learning, but classical v2e simulators are too slow for on-device pretraining. We present v2e-Lite, an 8-bit quantised frame-to-event simulator that runs at 540 fps on a Jetson Orin Nano while preserving 96% of the event-Jaccard fidelity of the original. We demonstrate downstream gains on three event-based tasks after pretraining with v2e-Lite-generated events.

title: A Decade of Event-Based Vision: A Survey of 1200 Papers
abstract: We survey the past decade of event-based vision, organising 1,200 papers across feature tracking, optical flow, reconstruction, depth, SLAM, segmentation, classification, control, datasets, simulators, and hardware. We trace 38 sub-trends, identify 14 open problems, and provide a dataset taxonomy with download links. This is the first community-driven update to the 2020 TPAMI survey and includes papers up to 2026.

title: Neuromorphic Tactile Sensing: A Roadmap
abstract: We outline a research roadmap for neuromorphic tactile sensing, drawing parallels to the trajectory of event-based vision. Twelve open challenges are presented around device fabrication, asynchronous read-out, learning algorithms, and benchmark datasets. The roadmap is the output of a 2-day workshop held at the 2026 ECCG meeting.

title: Differentiable Event Generation for End-to-End Closed-Loop Training
abstract: We make the v2e event generation process differentiable, enabling gradient flow from downstream task losses all the way through the synthetic event stream. We apply this to closed-loop optical-flow training and show a 1.4 EPE improvement over teacher-student baselines. Implementation in PyTorch released.
